html:
Exclusive to:

The Sea Strider Nest is a naturally spawning object exclusive to Don't Starve Together and introduced in the Waterlogged beta. It is the home of Sea Striders, and can be found in Waterlogged biome.

Sea Striders will emerge from the nest at Dusk by climbing down from above in a manner similar to Dangling Depth Dwellers. During the daytime any Sea Striders that are not engaged with an enemy or pursuing food will return to their nest of origin and despawn. Sea Striders will also emerge to investigate when a nearby Mossy Vine is picked or the Great Tree Trunk the nest is connected to is rammed by a boat.

The max amount of Sea Striders a Nest can have could be 2 or 3. It takes about 2 minutes for a Nest to respawn a new Sea Strider.

The Nest has 200 health and can be destroyed with weapons. Once a nest is destroyed, the Great Tree Trunk it was hanging on will spawn a new nest after 3 to 5 days. But a Great Tree Trunk can only regrow one nest at a time, so if another nest is destroyed when there is already a nest in regrowth, it will be destroyed permanently. If a nest catches on fire and burns down, the Great Tree Trunk will not regrow a new nest.


Gallery

  • Sea Strider Nest concept art from Rhymes With Play #282.[1]
  • Sea Strider Nest concept art from Rhymes With Play #282.
  • Sea Strider Nest concept art from Rhymes With Play #282.
  • Sea Strider Nest concept animation from Rhymes With Play #282.

References

  1. ↑ Rhymes With Play #282
DSTDSRoGSWHam

“What if I just gave it a little poke?”
–Wilson
“Ewww look at it just dangling up there!”
–Willow
“Wolfgang not like look of that...”
–Wolfgang
“Something sinister lurks above us.”
–Wendy
“CREEPY CRAWLIES DETECTED”
–WX-78
“This species seems to be both arboreal and aquatic.”
–Wickerbottom
“A part of me is just dying to poke it.”
–Woodie
“It looks like someone's already made themselves comfortable here.”
–Maxwell
“The nest öf the water walking beasts!”
–Wigfrid
“Oooh, can we come play in your tree house?”
–Webber
“Perhaps I'll just leave that be.”
–Warly
“Made house in friend”
–Wormwood
“I'm... trying not to think about how many of those are probably dangling above us.”
–Winona
“Rock-a-bye spiders, in the treetop...”
–Wortox
“Why Spiderfolk house in tree?”
–Wurt
“That must be their nest! If I could just get a closer look...”
–Walter
“As long as I don't get too close...”
–Wanda

The '''Sea Strider Nest''' is a naturally spawning object exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'' and introduced in the ''[[Don't_Starve_Together/Version_History#Waterlogged_Beta_.28since_July_26.2C_2021.29|Waterlogged]]'' beta. It is the home of [[Sea Strider|Sea Striders]], and can be found in [[Waterlogged]] biome.

Sea Striders will emerge from the nest at [[Day-Night Cycle|Dusk]] by climbing down from above in a manner similar to [[Dangling Depth Dweller|Dangling Depth Dwellers]]. During the daytime any Sea Striders that are not engaged with an enemy or pursuing food will return to their nest of origin and despawn. Sea Striders will also emerge to investigate when a nearby [[Mossy Vine]] is picked or the [[Great Tree Trunk]] the nest is connected to is rammed by a boat.

The max amount of Sea Striders a Nest can have could be 2 or 3. It takes about 2 minutes for a Nest to respawn a new Sea Strider.

The Nest has 200 health and can be destroyed with weapons. Once a nest is destroyed, the Great Tree Trunk it was hanging on will spawn a new nest after 3 to 5 days. But a Great Tree Trunk can only regrow one nest at a time, so if another nest is destroyed when there is already a nest in regrowth, it will be destroyed permanently. If a nest catches on fire and burns down, the Great Tree Trunk will not regrow a new nest.
